prompt: Put the new checkout behind a flag at 10%, then ramp it to 50%.
note: list to dedup, create the gate, then widen the rollout (ramp = update; there is no separate rollout tool)

prompt: Roll new checkout out to 50% of US users, always allow alice@acme.com and bob@acme.com, plus the internal team.
note: one gate, several gatekeeper steps: US 50% + email whitelist + team access. Args must carry country + whitelisted emails
terse ask must still geo-target India — list first to see if a payments gate exists, then create one (or enable/update the existing gate) scoped to India
read-only ask must not mutate anything

prompt: Set up a kill switch to disable the payments integration if it fails.
note: ASPIRATIONAL: after the kill switch, the agent should ask (AskUserQuestion) whether to add a failure metric + alert so we know WHEN to flip it

prompt: Set up a feature flag called dedupe_checkout for the new checkout page.
note: DEDUP: dedupe_checkout is pre-created; the ask would 'create' it again. This case is OUTCOME-gated — the point is that exactly one dedupe_checkout gate survives, however the agent gets there. Listing first is the ideal path (informational).
